title: Visit to Janatha Fishnet PVT. LTD Kundapura
content: The Department of Management studies organized an industrial visit to Janatha Fish Net Pvt Ltd, Dabbekatte for the first-year students on 20/12/2024 at 10:00 am.. Students were divided into two batches of 20 each and each batch was taken for the plant tour. Entire visit took 3 hours along with interaction of students with the manufacturing plant in-charge, research, and HR team.

title: Visit To Mahe Incubation Centre, Manipal
content: The MAHE Incubation Centre, part of the Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE), serves as a vibrant ecosystem designed to foster innovation and entrepreneurship among students and aspiring entrepreneurs. Established to support the development of start-ups, the centre provides a range of resources, mentorship, and networking opportunities, aimed at transforming ideas into viable business ventures. During our recent visit to the incubation centre, we were able to explore its state-of-the-art facilities, engage with experienced mentors, and gain insights into the entrepreneurial process.

title: Visit To Rainbow Pipe Factory
content: Rainbow Pipe Industry, located in Manipal, Udupi, is a leading manufacturer specializing in high-quality pipes and fittings for a variety of applications, including construction, plumbing, and industrial uses. With a strong commitment to innovation and sustainability, the company has integrated advanced manufacturing technologies and eco-friendly practices into its operations, setting a benchmark in the industry. During our recent visit to Rainbow Pipe Industry, we had the opportunity to explore their state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities, observe the production processes, and gain insights into the industry’s challenges and opportunities. This report aims to capture the key observations and learnings from our visit, emphasizing the company’s significance in the market and its contributions to sustainable development in the region.

title: Visit To Zonal Agricultural And Horticultural Research Station Brahmavara
content: On August 19, 2024, first-year MBA students visited the Zonal Agriculture and Horticultural Research Station in Brahmavara, Karnataka. This visit was part of an extension activity aimed at understanding the scope and impact of agricultural research. The research station's work in advancing agricultural practices and promoting sustainable farming provided us with valuable insights into how research contributes to improved crop productivity and resource management. Additionally, the visit highlighted potential entrepreneurial opportunities within the agricultural sector, such as Agri-tech innovations and enhancements in the agricultural supply chain. Engaging with the station’s operations offered us a clearer perspective on how these insights could inform and inspire future entrepreneurial endeavors in agriculture.

title: Visit To Vidya Academy
content: The students of 1st year MBA visited Academy, Moodlakatte which is a primary school from pre-kg to 2nd standard on 29th July 2024. MBA students conducted various pre-school activities such as organized academic activities, interactive story telling sessions, quiz. Children were involved in doing crafts, playing games, dance to enhance their skills. The initiative provided practical experience for the MBA students in managing and organizing educational activities, while also benefiting the children with enriching learning experiences.

title: Talk on Innovation & Design Thinking by Dr. Zubair, Chief Innovation Officer MAHE Innovation Centre
content: The session successfully provided MBA students with a deeper understanding of ideation, innovation and design thinking. Through Zubair’s engaging presentation and hands-on-prototype activity, students enhanced their creative problem-solving skills and gained practical experience. The interactive nature of the session not only increased students’ engagement but also fostered teamwork and offered valuable insights from an industry expert. Overall the event effectively combined theoretical knowledge with practical application, preparing students to apply these concepts in their future careers

title: Beach Cleaning Drive
content: As responsible citizens of our society, it is our duty to take meaningful action to protect and preserve our natural resources. Beaches hold immense ecological, cultural, and recreational significance, but they face increasing challenges from pollution and waste. Through this initiative, we aim to contribute toward restoring the beauty and health of our coastline while raising awareness about the importance of sustainable practices. Department of management studies organised a Beach Cleaning Drive to Malpe Beach on 25/ 01/ 2025. The beach cleaning activity took around 2 hours by the students and collected garbage handed over to Udupi municipal garbage collection truck at the end of the drive. Beach cleaning drive became successful with the collective effort of students, tourism department and municipal corporation of Udupi in restoring the beauty of Malpe beach and also a meaningful step toward protecting our environment and marine life. This drive has been a reminder of how small actions, when combined, can lead to significant change. Beyond cleaning the beach, we have raised awareness about the importance of reducing waste, recycling, and adopting sustainable habits.

title: Vibration Week
content: The much-anticipated Vibration Week was conducted from 27th August to 31st August 2024, with a series of exciting events designed to bring together both senior and junior MBA students. Nine teams were formed, combining talents from both batches, and each team had a dedicated leader. The event aimed to foster teamwork, creativity, and competition in a vibrant and fun environment, with students and faculty alike participating and showcasing their talents. Vibration Week 2024, held for MBA students, was a week filled with exciting activities that fostered collaboration and creativity. Nine teams, each consisting of both senior and junior students, participated in various events, with each team led by a team leader. The week began with a formal inauguration hosted by Sahara Shetty and Devendra and included a diverse range of competitions such as Retro Day, Quiz, Best Manager, Mismatch, PPT presentations, Fashion Show, Mad Ad, Rose Day, Singing, Cooking Without Fire, and Group Dance. Both students and faculty actively participated, showcasing their talents in a vibrant and lively manner

title: Water Day Celebration
content: The Water Day celebration at Moodalakatte Institute of Technology was held on 22nd March 2025. It helped the students to understand the importance of saving water through poster- making, dance, skit, and a documentary screening. The posters spread awareness with creative slogans and drawings. The dance performance showed the beauty of water and the dangers of pollution. The skit highlighted how careless water use leads to scarcity, and the documentary gave real-world examples of water crises. The event inspired students to use water wisely and take small steps to save it. It was a fun and educational experience, reminding everyone that every drop matter. Through posters, dance, skit, and a documentary screening, students not only learned about water conservation but also got inspired to make small changes in their daily lives. The event showed how wasting water can lead to serious problems and how simple actions, like turning off taps and using water wisely, can make a big difference. It also encouraged students to spread awareness among their friends and family. Overall, the session was a great way to remind everyone that water is precious and that it is our responsibility to protect it for the future. Every drop matter, and if we all work together, we can help save water and the environment!

title: Digital Marketing Workshop on 29 January 2025
content: On January 29, 2025, the Department of Management Studies organized a digital marketing workshop conducted by Dr. Nidhisha Rao, Assistant Professor at Justice K.S. Hegde Institute of Management, Nitte (Deemed to be University). In the workshop, Dr. Rao discussed key performance indicators in digital marketing, the meaning of marketing and digital marketing, and how every small business can benefit from digital marketing. He emphasized whether digital marketing should be a priority for a business & how it compares to traditional marketing. Dr. Nidhisha Rao also explained how to create a website, highlighting essential elements like the homepage, about us, contact page, product or service page, and social media integration. As part of the workshop, the students were tasked with finding a product to sell online, creating a brand name, designing a content page, using Canva to develop a poster and logo, selecting a website-building tool, and setting up a social media page for the product. Throughout the workshop, students were actively engaged and gained deeper knowledge about digital marketing.

title: Workshop on Awaken the Superhero
content: The Department of Management Studies, Moodlakatte Institute of Technology, Kundapura, organized an engaging and motivational workshop titled “Awaken the Superhero in You” on 5 December 2025 at 10:00 AM in the college auditorium. This workshop was conducted to enhance students’ personal development, confidence, leadership capabilities, and real-world decision-making skills. The workshop was skilfully delivered by the Resource Person – Mr. Ramani Venkat, Sales Strategic Consultant & Mentor to Startups. His diverse experience across industries greatly enriched the MBA students' learning environment. The event commenced with a warm welcome and a floral greeting to the resource person. Soon after the introduction, Mr. Ramani Venkat began his session by engaging students in energetic interactions and self-reflection activities. Throughout the workshop, he involved every student through group tasks, case-based learning, real-life corporate examples, and role-play leadership tasks. Students were encouraged to speak up, participate actively, and challenge their limitations. He focused on developing confidence, courage to face audiences, decision-making ability, and the power of a positive mindset. Inspiring stories and real business scenarios made the session lively and relatable, while each activity helped students push beyond their comfort zones. The hall was filled with laughter, learning, and active participation as students discovered unique strengths within themselves. The session concluded with students positively sharing their experiences and expressing how the workshop motivated them to bring out the best version of themselves. A vote of thanks was extended in appreciation of the resource person’s valuable time and impactful contribution.

title: Microsession On Entrepreneurship And Industry Trends
content: The Department of Management Studies, Moodlakatte Institute of Technology, Kundapura, organized a Micro session on Entrepreneurship and Industry Trends on 14th August 2025. The session was led by Mrs. Shruthi Dinkar, Founder & CEO of Avinya Talent, who shared her entrepreneurial journey and insights on the evolving business landscape. Faculty members, students, and aspiring entrepreneurs actively participated in this knowledge-sharing event. The micro session was highly informative and thought-provoking. It provided students with a clearer understanding of their career paths, future opportunities, and the challenges that may arise in the years to come. The interaction with students helped connect personally with the topic, encouraging them to reflect on their future while also motivating them to adopt an entrepreneurial mindset. Overall, the session successfully bridged the gap between classroom learning and real-world industry expectations, leaving students inspired and better prepared for the dynamic business world.

title: Session on AI Technology - 9th August 2025
content: The AI Technology Training Program was organized to equip participants with fundamental and advanced knowledge of Artificial Intelligence, its applications, and emerging trends. The AI Technology Training Program organized for the Kundapura Taluk Working Journalists’ Association is a forward-looking initiative designed to empower local journalists with essential knowledge of Artificial Intelligence. As the media industry rapidly embraces digital transformation, AI has become a powerful tool for news gathering, content creation, fact-checking, and audience engagement. This training aims to familiarize journalists with practical AI applications, ethical considerations, and innovative tools that can enhance reporting quality, speed, and accuracy. By bridging traditional journalism with modern technology, the program helps journalists in Kundapura Taluk stay competitive and relevant in today’s fast-paced news environment. On the 9th, at 10:30 AM, the program commenced in the presence of the Chief Guest, Kundapura Superintendent of Police (SP) Dr. S.D. Kulkarni, along with our College Chairman, Mr. Siddhartha J. Shetty, Circle Inspector Mr. Raghavendra Nayak, President of the Kundapura Taluk Working Journalists’ Association, Mr. Ramesh Suvarna, and other distinguished members of the association. The event began with a prayer, followed by a felicitation ceremony led by our Chairman. Several dignitaries, including Dr. S.D. Kulkarni, Mr. Raghavendra Nayak, and Mr. Ramesh Suvarna addressed the gathering, sharing their thoughts on the significance of the program. Subsequently, participants proceeded to the computer lab for a hands-on session on AI technology. The program concluded in the afternoon after the practical training session, leaving the participants enriched with knowledge and inspired by innovation.

title: Preplacement training- 21 July 2025 to 26 July 2025
content: The Department of Management Studies organized a comprehensive Pre-Placement Training Program to prepare Management students for the challenges of the recruitment process and to enhance their employability skills. This program was organized by the Department of Management Studies in collaboration with the Iskew Learning Team, was designed to bridge the gap between academic knowledge and industry expectations The Pre-Placement Training Program took place from 21 July 2025 to 26 July 2025 at LHO03. Over six days, the Iskew Learning Team conducted different sessions covering all stages of recruitment. Students were taught how to create a good resume, improve personal branding, and develop effective communication skills through public speaking, role-plays, and presentations. Aptitude and logical reasoning practice helped them improve problem solving skills. Group discussion sessions taught students how to share ideas clearly and confidently. Mock interviews, both HR and technical, gave students real interview experience and useful feedback. Trainers also spoke about workplace culture, time management, and stress management. The sessions were interactive, practical, and enjoyed by students. By the end of the week, students felt more confident and prepared for placements. The Pre-Placement Training Program achieved its objective of preparing students for competitive recruitment processes. The Iskew Learning Team effectively delivered valuable insights and practical tips, fostering readiness and confidence among participants. The enthusiastic participation throughout the week reflected the students’ determination to excel in their careers. This initiative is expected to positively impact placement outcomes for the academic year.

title: A session on navigating the digital frontier
content: On 2 July 2025, the Department of Management Studies organised a session on “Navigating the Digital Frontier: The Future of Tech-Driven Enterprises” for the first-year students of MBA, conducted by Mr. Anish B., Business Analyst, Tietoevry, Bengaluru. The event served as an introductory session to “Navigating the Digital Frontier: The Future of Tech-Driven Enterprises”. The speaker talked about how technology is changing the way companies work today and in the future. He explained how tools like artificial intelligence, cloud systems, and data are helping businesses grow and work smarter, and briefed on how to work in the IT sector as well as what are the key points to gain knowledge about IT sector, how IT companies solve the business problems and how they are adopting tactics in business to reach the goal and earning profits. He also shared about the challenges companies face while using new technology and why it is important to be open to change, learn new things, and think ahead to succeed in this fast-moving digital world. The speaker explained important concepts like HR executive, finance executive, business analyst, marketing executive, and other concepts. While explaining the concepts, the speaker gave many examples for understanding purposes, which are more beneficial for understanding the concepts. “Navigating the Digital Frontier: The Future of Tech-Driven Enterprises” gave a clear understanding of how technology is shaping the future of businesses. It highlighted the importance of staying updated with digital trends and being open to change. The session also encouraged students to learn new skills and use technology in smart ways to grow and succeed. Overall, it was an informative and helpful session for understanding the role of today’s business world.

title: Industrial Visit to Bright Flexi International Limited, Padubidri- 29/09/2025
content: An industrial visit was organized by the Department of Management Studies for the second-year MBA students on 29th September 2025 to Bright Flexi International Limited, Padubidri, to provide students with practical exposure to real-world industrial processes and operations. The purpose of the visit was to understand the functioning, production process, management practices, and safety standards maintained in the industry The primary objective of this visit was to understand the production processes, supply chain mechanisms, quality control systems, and overall management practices that contribute to the efficiency and competitiveness of the organization. It also enabled students to observe the integration of technology, human resources, and innovation in achieving production goals. Through this experience, MBA students gained practical insights into how managerial decisions are implemented on the production floor, how resources are optimized, and how production strategies align with broader business objectives. This visit served as an essential learning platform, enhancing students’ understanding of industrial operations and preparing them for managerial roles in dynamic business environments

title: Industrial Visit to Micro-finish Valves Pvt. Ltd. (Hubballi) and Nemus Pharmaceuticals Pvt. Ltd. (Dharwad) – 4 to 6 February 2025
content: An industrial visit was organized for MBA students to two renowned industries—Microfinish Valves Pvt. Ltd. in Hubballi and Nemus Pharmaceuticals Pvt. Ltd. in Dharwad. This visit aimed to provide first-hand experience and practical insights into these prominent companies' manufacturing processes, operations, and business functions. The group was accompanied by MBA faculty members and spent the day observing the workings of the industries, learning about their operational strategies, and engaging with professionals The industrial visit to Microfinish Valves Pvt. Ltd. and Nemus Pharmaceuticals Pvt. Ltd. was an enriching and educational experience for all MBA students. It provided a deeper understanding of the manufacturing and pharmaceutical industries, highlighting the importance of quality, innovation, and adherence to industry standards. The visit not only enhanced students’ theoretical knowledge but also offered practical insights into business operations and strategies. It was a valuable learning experience that contributed to the overall growth and development of the students, bridging the gap between academic learning and real-world industrial practices.

title: Faculty Development Programme FDP on “AI in Research & Empowering Researchers with Ethical Insights & Academic Excellence”- 24th – 28th November 2025
content: Research a vital role in the progress of society, academia, and industry. It provides a systematic and scientific approach to discovering new facts, verifying existing knowledge, and solving real-world problems. In today’s rapidly changing academic environment, faculty members need to possess sound research skills and methodological expertise to conduct high-quality research. The Faculty Development Programme (FDP) on AI Research & Empowering Researchers with Ethical Insights & Academic Excellence is designed to enhance the research competencies of faculty members, researchers, and scholars across various disciplines. The Faculty Development Programme proved to be a valuable initiative for improving faculty competence and academic quality. The sessions contributed significantly to professional development and equipped faculty members with the skills needed for effective teaching and impactful research. It is recommended that similar programmes be conducted periodically to ensure continuous growth and upskilling of faculty members. The FDP’s success was backed by the key dignitaries of the program, including Dr. Ramakrishna Hegde- Principal, MITK & Advisor of the programme, Dr. Suchithra Poojari- HOD & Convenor of the programme, Ms. Kavya, Mr. Neil Gladwin Dlima, Ms. Poornima, Mrs. Thilaka Laxmi, Mrs. Nagaveni, and Ms. Chaithali, Coordinators of the Programme, and all the other members of MITK.

title: WORKSHOP ON FINANCIAL PLANNING FOR YOUNG PROFESSIONALS: BUILDING A STRONG FOUNDATION- 26th & 27th September 2025
content: The Department of Management Studies organized a two-day workshop titled “Financial Planning for Young Professionals: Building a Strong Foundation” in collaboration with the National Institute of Securities Markets (NISM). The two-day workshop on Financial Planning for Young Professionals was conducted on 26th September 2025 and 27th September 2025, facilitated by resource person Mr. Steevan Robert Tellis from NISM. The session began with an introduction to the importance of financial discipline and smart money management in the early stages of one’s career. Participants learned about budgeting techniques, emergency fund planning, insurance needs, and retirement savings. Trainers explained the basics of mutual funds, systematic investment plans (SIPs), equity and debt instruments, and the role of SEBI in investor protection. The workshop included interactive activities, quizzes, and scenario-based discussions to help students apply financial concepts practically. Real-world examples and success stories of young investors made the session highly engaging. Students also gained awareness about common financial mistakes and how to avoid them. The event concluded with a Q&A session, where students clarified doubts related to investments and financial planning for different career stages Overall, the workshop on Financial Planning for Young Professionals conducted by NISM was highly informative and impactful. It successfully achieved its objective of fostering financial awareness and preparing students to handle personal finances wisely. The expert-led session motivated participants to plan their finances early, avoid debt traps, and invest systematically. The Department of Management Studies expressed gratitude to NISM for its valuable contribution in empowering students with essential financial knowledge, which will significantly benefit them in their personal and professional lives.

title: Short movie competition – 29/11/2025
content: The Short-Movie Making Competition held on 29 November 2025 began with an enthusiastic briefing, setting a lively tone for the event. Students from both Section A and Section B were introduced to the theme “College Life,” after which each group immediately immersed itself in planning, scripting, and organizing its short films. Throughout the activity, every student participated actively—whether through acting, scriptwriting, cinematography, direction, choreography, or editing. The event encouraged students to express creativity while working collaboratively, pushing them to explore innovative ways of blending comedy, horror, moral lessons, and real-life situations. The preparation phase was filled with teamwork, excitement, and imaginative thinking as students stepped out of their comfort zones and discovered new talents. During the screening, the auditorium was filled with laughter, appreciation, and curiosity as each group presented its unique interpretation of the theme. Judges Mr. Karthikeyan from the Department of Data Science and Mr. Kiran from the Admission department evaluated the films based on creativity, storyline, technical skills, teamwork, choreography, and overall execution. Their constructive feedback added great value to the learning experience. The event concluded with students sharing positive reflections on how the competition boosted their confidence, creativity, coordination, and communication skills, making it a memorable and enriching experience for everyone. Overall the Short-Movie Making Competition was a fun, engaging, and meaningful activity for the students of the Department of Management Studies. It helped students improve their creativity, teamwork, and confidence while learning the basics of making a short film. The department thanked the management and the judges, Mr. Karthikeyan and Mr. Kiran, for their support and guidance throughout the event. The competition ended on a positive note, with students feeling encouraged, motivated, and excited to take part in more such learning activities in the future.

title: Freshers Day- 03rd and 4th December 2025
content: Department of Management 2025Studies organised day on 3rd & 4th December. Freshers were given an opportunity to introduce themselves and participate in fun activities and games. Interactive sessions helped reduce nervousness and encouraged confidence among the newcomers. Several rounds were conducted to identify talents and promote teamwork. One of the highlights of the event was the selection of Mr. and Ms. Fresher, based on confidence, talent, communication skills, and personality. The winners were felicitated with titles and prizes. Freshers’ Day was a grand success and served as an excellent platform for new students to interact, express themselves, and feel welcomed. The event strengthened the bond between students and faculty and marked a positive beginning to the academic journey of the freshers.

title: BIZ QUIZ 2025
content: Department of Management Studies organised “Biz Quiz” Inter-College Quiz Competition on 02/06/2025 in the college Auditorium. For this event, students from different colleges were invited to participate, and Prof. Neil Gladwin Dlima, Assistant Professor, took charge as Quiz Master. The entire quiz was designed in 2 rounds (Prelims and Final).

title: World No Tobacco Day on 31st May 2025.
content: On 31st May 2025, the Department of Management Studies at Moodlakatte Institute of Technology organized the World No Tobacco Day celebration with the theme-Eliminate Tobacco from Your Life Before It Kills You-The event focused on raising awareness about the harmful effects of tobacco, especially among young students. A powerful and thought-provoking skit was the highlight of the program, performed by the 1st-year students of the MBA. The skit portrayed a realistic college-life scenario where students get influenced by the pressure of friends and gradually fall into the habit of tobacco use. It effectively showed how such habits develop and the negative consequences they bring to health and academics. The skit was presented to an audience comprising students from various degree colleges, who were invited to attend the event. The engaging performance captured the attention of everyone present and delivered a strong message about resisting pressure and making healthy lifestyle choices. The event successfully encouraged self-reflection and awareness among students about the dangers of tobacco. It served as a reminder that staying informed and strong-minded can help them lead a better, tobacco-free life.

title: World Environment Day- 05th June  2025
content: On 31st May 2025, the Department of Management Studies at Moodlakatte Institute of Technology organized the World No Tobacco Day celebration with the theme-Eliminate Tobacco from Your Life Before It Kills You-The event focused on raising awareness about the harmful effects of tobacco, especially among young students. A powerful and thought-provoking skit was the highlight of the program, performed by the 1st-year students of the MBA. The skit portrayed a realistic college-life scenario where students get influenced by the pressure of friends and gradually fall into the habit of tobacco use. It effectively showed how such habits develop and the negative consequences they bring to health and academics. The skit was presented to an audience comprising students from various degree colleges, who were invited to attend the event. The engaging performance captured the attention of everyone present and delivered a strong message about resisting pressure and making healthy lifestyle choices. The event successfully encouraged self-reflection and awareness among students about the dangers of tobacco. It served as a reminder that staying informed and strong-minded can help them lead a better, tobacco-free life.

title: INTERNATIONAL YOGA DAY – 20th June 2025
content: On 20 June 2025, the Department of Management Studies organised a yoga session as a part of the International Yoga Day for the First-Year students of MBA, conducted by Yoga instructor Manjunath S. Bijoor, Head Teacher at Government Higher Primary School, Bijoor. The event served as an introductory session to yoga, highlighting its importance for physical and mental well-being. Manjunath S. Bijoor guided participants through the basic asanas such as vajrasana, bhujangasana, shavasana, breathing exercises and meditation techniques, and their benefits. The session was informative, interactive, and well-received by students by promoting the culture of wellness and mindfulness in the college environment. Throughout the session, students were actively engaged and gained knowledge about yoga. the yoga session fulfilled its objective of promoting wellness and aligning with the spirit of International Yoga Day. Manjunath S. Bijoor effectively introduced the basics of yoga to the participants and highlighted its physical, mental, and emotional benefits regarding each of the asanas being shown. Also, the enthusiastic involvement of students reflected the growing interest in healthy living. It also motivated many to incorporate yoga into their daily routine.

title: ORIENTATION PROGRAMME- 4th November 2025
content: The Orientation Programme organized by the Department of PG Studies (MBA & MCA), Moodlakatte Institute of Technology, Kundapura, was held on 4th November 2025 at 10:00 AM in the college auditorium. The programme was organized to welcome the new batch of MBA and MCA students and to introduce them to the institution’s environment, academic culture, and opportunities ahead. The Chief Guest for the programme was Mr. B.S. Suresh Shetty, Founder & Managing Director of Sumukha Surgicals India Pvt. Ltd., Uppunda. The event was attended by respected dignitaries, including the Principal, Vice Principal, Head of Department, Faculty members, and Students. The orientation aimed to motivate and guide students as they begin their new academic journey, setting a tone of inspiration, learning, and growth. The Orientation Programme began with a warm welcome address by the faculty, followed by the lighting of the lamp ceremony, symbolizing knowledge and enlightenment. Dignitaries and faculty members graced the event with their presence and words of wisdom. The highlight of the programme was the motivational address by the Chief Guest, Mr. B.S. Suresh Shetty, who shared his entrepreneurial journey and life lessons. He inspired the students to be confident, disciplined, and goal-oriented. His words encouraged everyone to develop leadership qualities and contribute positively to society. The principal and other dignitaries also addressed the gathering, emphasizing the importance of time management, teamwork, and continuous learning. They guided students on how to make the best use of academic and co-curricular opportunities during their course. The programme also included an interactive session where new students could express their expectations and interact with faculty members. The event concluded with a vote of thanks, appreciating the efforts of the management, faculty, and organizing committee for making the event successful.

title: Vibration Week-  9th to 12th September 2025
content: The Department of Management Studies at Moodlakatte Institute of Technology, Kundapura, organized “Vibration Week – 2025” from 9th to 12th September 2025 for MBA students. This event was considered a Management and Cultural Event, aimed at promoting creativity, leadership, team spirit, and managerial aptitude among students. The week-long celebrations provided a balanced mix of academic, cultural, and fun-filled activities, allowing participants to explore their latent talents while learning practical management lessons. The week commenced on 9th September with a grand inauguration, followed by a Flash Mob, creating an energetic start to the event. The day included Mind Heist (a Quiz) that tested general knowledge, management concepts, and current affairs, as well as Clue Crew (a Treasure Hunt) that challenged participants’ problem-solving and teamwork abilities. On 10th September, the focus shifted to creative and presentation skills. Speakathon (Presentation) allowed students to deliver topics related to management trends, innovation, and social awareness, enhancing their public speaking confidence. Reel Karo Feel Karo (Reel Making) encouraged students to produce short, engaging videos that combined creativity with storytelling, promoting digital literacy and content creation skills. 11th September featured Master Mind (Best Manager), an event designed to assess leadership, decision-making, and strategic thinking under competitive scenarios. Kitchen King (Cooking Without Fire) tested creativity, planning, and execution skills in a fun and engaging way, promoting teamwork and innovation outside the conventional classroom setup. The final day, 12th September, concluded with Ashtarang Utsav, a cultural extravaganza where eight teams showcased dance, singing, mad ads, and fashion shows. Each team performed for 25 minutes, and the audience and judges appreciated the energy, synchronization, and originality of performances. This event brought together all participants, celebrating talent and teamwork in a grand finale.

title: YUVA 2025
content: YUVA FEST 2025, the much-anticipated intercollegiate fest organized by MITK, brought together students from 16 colleges for an exciting and competitive celebration of culture, intellect, and creativity. The event provided a dynamic platform for young talents to showcase their skills in both management and cultural competitions The fest was hosted by Sahara Shetty and Shambavi, who ensured an engaging and lively atmosphere throughout the event. The presence of Mr. Nitin Hegde, the esteemed Chief Guest, added prestige to the occasion, as he inspired participants with his insightful words during the inauguration ceremony. YUVA FEST 2025 featured a diverse array of events, including Best Manager, Quiz, Mad Ad, Marketing, Photography, and YUVA Parampara (Cultural Showcase), offering a stage for students to demonstrate their leadership, innovation, and artistic brilliance. After an intense series of competitions, BB Hegde College emerged as the overall champions, securing top positions in multiple events. The fest concluded with a grand valedictory ceremony, celebrating the hard work and enthusiasm of participants, faculty, and the organizing team. The event successfully fostered collaboration, competitiveness, and a sense of community among students, making it a remarkable and memorable experience. YUVA FEST 2025 was a resounding success, bringing together students from different backgrounds to celebrate their talents. The event not only strengthened inter-college relationships but also highlighted the organizing capabilities of MITK’s students. The fest received positive feedback from participants and faculty members, making it a memorable experience for everyone involved. This initiative will continue to inspire and set a benchmark for future editions of YUVA FEST.

title: Personal Leadership Training Program
content: The Department of Management Studies organised an ISR activity on “Personal Leadership Training” in collaboration with “FSL INDIA” for the First-year students of MBA on 12/01/2026 and 13/01/2026 at 10:00 am, by the Program co-ordinator, Asst. Prof. Thilaka Laxmi and Joint co-ordinator Asst. Prof. Chaithali. Mr.Rakesh Soans, President, an activist, FSL India, and his team, designed the entire session to engage students mentally and physically to boost critical thinking ability to contribute towards problems facing society.

title: 3 Days Industrial Visit to NestTech Park
content: The Department of Management Studies organised an Industrial Visit to Kerala (Kochi) for first-year and second-year MBA students from 18/01/2026 to 21/01/2026. A total of 42 students, along with 4 faculty members, participated in the visit. As part of the industrial exposure, students visited NestTech Park, Kochi, an IT and technology hub that supports software companies, startups, and training centres. The company representatives explained about the organisation structure, IT services, project management, teamwork, and the importance of technology in modern business operations. Students gained insights into corporate culture, professional ethics, and career opportunities in IT and management fields. In addition to the industrial visit, students were taken for sightseeing to Marine Drive Beach, Guruvayur Shri Krishna Temple, Athirappilly Waterfalls, and Lulu Mall. These visits provided exposure to tourism management .

title: Intra College Competition on Budget Analysis 2026
content: The Department of Management Studies, Moodlakatte Institute of Technology, organized an Intra College Competition on “Budget Analysis 2026” for MBA students on 06/02/2026 at 09:30 AM in the Auditorium. The event was evaluated by Dr. Subramanya A, Professor of Economics, Government First Grade College, Kota, who has over 18 years of academic experience and was awarded his Ph.D. by Mangalore University in 2018 for his research on fiscal performance of the Union Government after the FRBM Act, 2003. With 30 research publications and presentations at 16 national and international conferences, he brought strong expertise in fiscal policy and macroeconomic analysis to the evaluation process. His academic contributions and experience in research, institutional quality enhancement, and career development added significant value to the competition.

title: Report on Value Addition Course In Collaboration with ISKEW Learning Team
content: The Department of Management Studies, Moodlakatte Institute of Technology, Kundapura, organized a one-week intensive training programme on Aptitude, Excel, and Power BI for MBA students from 15th December 2025 to 21st December 2025 at LH003. The training was conducted in collaboration with the ISKEW Learning Team to strengthen students’ analytical, quantitative, and data-handling skills essential for placements and corporate readiness. The programme featured two expert trainers: Mr. Nageswara Rao, who handled the Aptitude training sessions, and Ms. Asha Sukumaran, who conducted the Excel and Power BI sessions over four days. The training was designed to be highly interactive, practical, and application-oriented, ensuring active student participation throughout the week.

title: International Plastic Bag Free Day
content: International Plastic Bag Free Day was organised in the Department of Management Studies (MBA) on 3rd July through an awareness initiative aimed at reducing the use of single-use plastics. As part of the program, students and staff actively participated in distributing reusable cloth bags to local households in Koni, Moodlakatte, and Basrur. This effort was designed to promote sustainable habits and encourage the community to shift towards eco-friendly alternatives. It teaches the importance of reducing plastic use and protecting nature. By taking part in activities like giving out cloth bags or attending awareness programs, students learn teamwork, responsibility, and how small actions can make a big difference. It also inspires them to lead an eco-friendlier life and influence others to do the same. The event not only highlighted the harmful effects of plastic pollution but also emphasized the importance of individual and collective action in protecting the environment.

title: Expert Talk on  Human Resource Management by Mr. Devaraju Ramu
content: The highlight of the programme was the talk delivered by Mr. Devaraju Ramu, who provided an in-depth understanding of Human Resource Management and its importance in modern organizations. He explained how HR is not just about recruitment and payroll but also about building strong relationships, fostering a positive work environment, and aligning employee goals with organizational vision. He discussed real- life industry scenarios, emphasizing the skills required for HR professionals, such as communication, emotional intelligence, adaptability, and leadership. The session also covered topics like talent acquisition, employee engagement, and performance management. His examples from corporate life made the discussion practical and relatable for students. The session concluded with an interactive Q&A, where students asked questions about HR careers, interview preparation, and workplace challenges. The interaction helped bridge the gap between academic learning and corporate reality. A vote of thanks was delivered by a student representative, expressing gratitude to the resource person, faculty, and management for organizing such an insightful session.

title: Parent–Teacher Meeting
content: The Department of Management Studies, Moodlakatte Institute of Technology, Kundapura, successfully organized a Parent–Teacher Meeting on 27th December 2025 at 09:30 AM in the college Auditorium. The meeting witnessed active participation from parents, faculty members, and department representatives. Dr Suchitra Poojari, Head of the Department, discussed students’ academic performance, internal assessment results, attendance records, behavioural aspects, and skill development initiatives. Parents were encouraged to share their feedback, concerns, and suggestions regarding their ward’s progress. The interaction helped create a transparent and supportive academic environment, ensuring that both parents and teachers work together for the students’ success.

title: Idea Pitch Day
content: The session commenced with an overview of the importance of startups and innovation in today’s competitive business environment. MBA students actively participated by presenting their innovative startup ideas, focusing on problem identification, unique solutions, target markets, and potential growth opportunities. Each presentation was followed by interactive discussions, where Mr. Adithya Poojary provided constructive feedback and practical suggestions. He clarified students’ queries related to feasibility, execution strategies, and market challenges. The session remained lively and engaging, encouraging students to ask questions and exchange ideas freely. The Startup Pitch created a collaborative learning environment where students gained hands- on experience in pitching, critical thinking, and decision-making. The interaction helped students understand how theoretical knowledge can be applied in practical business situations.
